bandResolutionRules.condition: No danger signs across all questions
bandResolutionRules.action: Home care. Routine follow-up per module schedule. Counsel on warning signs. Record visit on ANMOL.

bandResolutionRules.condition: Moderate risk — one or more YELLOW signs, no RED
bandResolutionRules.action: Refer to PHC within 24 h. Follow up in 2 days. Counsel on escalation signs. Record on ANMOL.
Refer FRU/SNCU/DH immediately (≤ 30–60 min). Call 108. Bengali action card displayed. Decision trace locked and stored. MDSR hook triggered if applicable.

questions.text: শিশু বুকের দুধ খেতে পারছে না?
questions.textEn: Is the baby unable to breastfeed?
questions.action: PSBI hard-stop. Refer to SNCU/FRU immediately (≤ 30 min). Do not delay for any reason.

questions.textEn: Does the baby have fever?
questions.action: PSBI hard-stop. Refer to SNCU/FRU immediately. Any fever in a newborn is a danger sign.

questions.textEn: Is breathing fast or difficult?
questions.action: PSBI hard-stop. Refer to SNCU/FRU immediately. Count RR — if ≥ 60/min = severe.

questions.text: নাভি লাল, ফোলা বা পুঁজ আছে?
questions.textEn: Is the navel red, swollen, or has pus?
questions.action: Refer to FRU/DH immediately. Omphalitis can progress to sepsis within hours.

questions.text: শিশু অনেক কম নড়ছে বা নিস্তেজ?
questions.textEn: Is the baby moving very little or lethargic?
questions.action: PSBI hard-stop. Refer to SNCU/FRU immediately. Lethargy = severe systemic illness.

questions.textEn: Does the skin look yellow or bluish?
questions.riskNote: Cyanosis = RED always. Jaundice within 24 h of birth or palms/soles yellow = RED. Jaundice day 2–14 without palm/sole = YELLOW.
questions.action: Cyanosis or jaundice within 24 h = refer SNCU immediately. Jaundice day 2–14 without palm/sole involvement = refer PHC within 24 h for bilirubin check.

questions.text: সব প্রশ্নের উত্তর না — কোনো বিপদচিহ্ন নেই?
questions.textEn: All questions answered NO — no danger signs?
questions.action: HBNC home visit schedule: day 1, 3, 7, 14, 28. Counsel on exclusive breastfeeding, warmth, cord care, danger sign recognition.

questions.textEn: Has fever lasted more than 5 days?
questions.action: Refer to PHC/DH same day. Rule out malaria, typhoid, dengue. Do not give antipyretics and send home.

questions.textEn: Is there cough or breathing difficulty?
questions.riskNote: Chest indrawing or stridor = RED. RR ≥ 50/min (2–12 m) or ≥ 40/min (1–5 y) = YELLOW.
questions.action: Count respiratory rate. RR ≥ 50 (2–12 m) or ≥ 40 (1–5 y) = pneumonia → PHC within 24 h. Chest indrawing or stridor = severe pneumonia → RED, refer FRU immediately.

questions.textEn: Is there diarrhoea or repeated vomiting?
questions.action: Assess dehydration. No dehydration = GREEN (ORS at home). Some dehydration = YELLOW (ORS at PHC). Severe dehydration (sunken eyes, skin pinch slow, unable to drink) = RED, IV fluids at FRU.

questions.textEn: Is the child completely refusing to eat?
questions.action: Assess for illness cause. If associated with fever/diarrhoea = escalate to relevant rule. Isolated feeding refusal > 2 days = PHC referral within 24 h.

questions.textEn: Signs of dehydration — sunken eyes, dry lips?
questions.action: Severe dehydration = RED. Start ORS immediately. Refer to FRU for IV fluids. Do not delay referral.

questions.textEn: Is weight much less than expected for age?
questions.action: Measure MUAC. MUAC < 11.5 cm or oedema = SAM → RED, refer NRC. MUAC 11.5–12.5 cm = MAM → YELLOW, refer ICDS/NRC for RUTF. MUAC > 12.5 cm = GREEN, counsel on feeding.

questions.text: শিশু খাওয়া-দাওয়া একদম বন্ধ করে দিয়েছে?
questions.textEn: Has the infant completely stopped feeding?
questions.action: Any feeding refusal in infant < 2 months = RED hard-stop. Refer FRU immediately.

questions.textEn: Has fever lasted more than 3 days?
symptoms: fever lasting more than 3 days in infant
questions.action: Refer to PHC/FRU same day. Infants < 3 months with any fever = RED hard-stop.

questions.textEn: Is there diarrhoea or repeated vomiting?
questions.action: Assess dehydration. Some dehydration = YELLOW, PHC within 24 h. Severe dehydration = RED, FRU for IV fluids.

questions.textEn: Is there breathing difficulty or chest sounds?
questions.action: Chest indrawing or stridor in infant = RED, refer FRU immediately. Fast breathing alone (RR ≥ 50) = YELLOW, refer PHC within 24 h.

questions.textEn: Signs of dehydration — sunken eyes, dry lips?
questions.action: Severe dehydration in infant = RED. Start ORS immediately. Refer FRU for IV fluids.

questions.text: ওজন বাড়ছে না বা কমে যাচ্ছে?
questions.textEn: Is weight not increasing or decreasing?
questions.action: Refer to ICDS/AWC for growth monitoring. If MUAC < 11.5 cm = RED, refer NRC. Counsel on complementary feeding after 6 months.

questions.text: রক্তচাপ বেশি বা মাথা ব্যথা হচ্ছে?
questions.textEn: Is blood pressure high or is there headache?
questions.action: Pre-eclampsia/eclampsia hard-stop. Refer to FRU/DH immediately. Do not give food/water. Lay on left side during transport.

questions.textEn: Is there swelling of legs or face?
questions.riskNote: Oedema + headache + high BP = RED. Isolated leg oedema = YELLOW.
questions.action: If oedema + headache + BP high = RED (pre-eclampsia). Isolated leg oedema = YELLOW, refer PHC within 24 h for BP check and urine protein.

questions.textEn: Is there bleeding or severe abdominal pain?
questions.action: APH hard-stop. Refer to FRU/DH immediately. Any bleeding after 20 weeks = emergency. Do not do vaginal examination.

questions.textEn: Has the baby's movement reduced?
questions.action: Refer to FRU/DH same day for fetal monitoring (CTG/Doppler). Absent fetal movement = emergency.

questions.text: তিন মাসের বেশি ANC চেকআপ বাদ গেছে?
questions.textEn: Has ANC checkup been missed for more than 3 months?
questions.action: Schedule ANC visit at PHC within 7 days. Check MCP card. Ensure TT, IFA, calcium supplementation are up to date. Flag for PMSMA if HRP.

questions.text: চোখে ঝাপসা বা মাথা ঘোরা হচ্ছে?
questions.textEn: Is there blurred vision or dizziness?
questions.action: Eclampsia prodrome hard-stop. Refer to FRU/DH immediately. Blurred vision + headache + oedema = imminent eclampsia.

questions.text: সব প্রশ্নের উত্তর না — কোনো বিপদচিহ্ন নেই?
questions.textEn: All questions answered NO — no danger signs?
questions.action: Routine ANC follow-up. Ensure 4 ANC visits (8, 14, 28, 36 weeks). Counsel on IFA (180 tabs), calcium (360 tabs), TT2, diet, birth preparedness. Register on ANMOL.

questions.textEn: Is there excessive bleeding or foul-smelling discharge?
questions.signOffNote: PPH proxy '≥ 2 pads soaked in 30 min' pending clinical sign-off (README open item #3).
questions.action: PPH hard-stop. Refer to FRU/DH immediately. If trained: bimanual uterine compression. Misoprostol 600 mcg sublingual if within ASHA scope (confirm at sign-off). Do not delay transport.

questions.textEn: Is there fever or chills?
questions.riskNote: Fever + foul discharge = RED (sepsis). Fever > 38°C after day 2 = RED. Isolated fever day 1–2 = YELLOW.
questions.action: Puerperal sepsis risk. Fever + foul discharge = RED. Isolated fever day 1–2 = YELLOW, refer PHC within 24 h. Fever > 38°C after day 2 = RED.

questions.text: স্তনে ব্যথা, ফোলা বা লাল দাগ?
questions.textEn: Is there breast pain, swelling, or red marks?
questions.riskNote: Fluctuant abscess = RED. Mastitis without abscess = YELLOW.
questions.action: Mastitis: continue breastfeeding, warm compress, refer PHC within 24 h for antibiotics. If abscess (fluctuant swelling) = RED, refer FRU for I&D.

questions.text: পেটে তীব্র ব্যথা বা সেলাইয়ে সমস্যা?
questions.textEn: Is there severe abdominal pain or suture problem?
questions.riskNote: Wound dehiscence or peritonitis signs = RED. Wound infection alone = YELLOW.
questions.action: Wound infection = YELLOW, refer PHC within 24 h. Wound dehiscence or severe pain = RED, refer FRU. Peritonitis signs (rigid abdomen, rebound tenderness) = RED hard-stop.

questions.textEn: Is there burning or difficulty urinating?
questions.action: UTI: refer PHC within 24 h for urine culture and antibiotics. If fever + flank pain = pyelonephritis → RED, refer FRU.

questions.text: খুব দুর্বল বা মাথা ঘোরা হচ্ছে?
questions.textEn: Is there extreme weakness or dizziness?
questions.action: Assess pallor (conjunctiva, palms). Severe pallor = anaemia → YELLOW, refer PHC for Hb check. Hb < 7 g/dL or fainting = RED, refer FRU for transfusion.

questions.textEn: What is the child's age in months?
questions.action: Schedule catch-up at nearest session site within 7 days. BCG can be given up to 1 year. OPV/IPV/Pentavalent: give missed dose, continue schedule. Do not restart series.

questions.textEn: Which vaccine was missed?
symptoms: JE dose 1 missed in endemic districts
questions.action: Schedule catch-up within 7 days. Measles-1 at 9 months, Vitamin A 1 lakh IU at 9 months. If > 12 months, give MMR instead of measles.

questions.text: কতদিন আগে টিকা দেওয়ার কথা ছিল?
questions.textEn: How many days ago was the vaccine due?
symptoms: vaccine overdue by more than 3 months
questions.action: Catch-up immunisation. Do not restart series. Give missed dose and continue from where left off. Refer to PHC for catch-up session. Flag on ANMOL for tracking.

questions.text: শিশুর কি কোনো অসুস্থতা আছে এখন?
questions.textEn: Does the child have any illness now?
questions.riskNote: Mild illness = vaccinate as scheduled. Moderate/severe illness = defer until recovered.
questions.action: Mild illness (mild fever, cold) = vaccinate as scheduled. Moderate/severe illness = defer vaccination until recovered. Reschedule within 7 days of recovery. Do not miss opportunity for mild illness.

questions.text: ১-৫ বছর বয়সী শিশুর বুস্টার ডোজ মিস হয়েছে?
questions.textEn: Has the booster dose been missed for child aged 1–5 years?
questions.action: DPT booster at 16–24 months. OPV booster at 16–24 months. MMR at 15–18 months. Vitamin A 6-monthly up to 5 years. Schedule catch-up at next session.

questions.textEn: Is there excessive bleeding?
questions.action: Hard-stop. Call 108 immediately. Refer FRU/DH ≤ 30 min. Apply uterine compression if postpartum. Do not leave patient alone.

questions.textEn: Has there been convulsion or loss of consciousness?
questions.action: Hard-stop. Lay on left side, protect airway. Call 108. Refer FRU/DH immediately. If eclampsia: MgSO4 loading dose if within ANM/MO scope. ASHA: do not delay transport.

questions.textEn: Is there severe difficulty breathing?
questions.action: Hard-stop. Call 108. Refer FRU/DH ≤ 30 min. Keep airway clear. If newborn: stimulate, clear airway, bag-mask if available.

questions.text: রোগী সাড়া দিচ্ছে না বা জ্ঞান নেই?
questions.textEn: Is the patient unresponsive or unconscious?
questions.action: Hard-stop. Call 108 immediately. Check airway, breathing, circulation. Recovery position. Refer FRU/DH ≤ 30 min. MDSR audit trigger if death occurs.

questions.textEn: Is any single hard-stop danger sign present?
questions.invariantNote: Re-sweep invariant (EM-005): once any hard-stop fires, band is locked RED. No subsequent answer can downgrade it. Engine must enforce this structurally.
questions.action: System invariant: RED band locked. No GREEN or YELLOW answer can downgrade a fired RED. Re-sweep confirms RED on every state update.
